summary: Set 068 Session 4 of 6: Routed keep / demote / retire decision. Ran on claude-opus-4-8 high (S3 routed rec was sonnet-4-6/medium, but the S3 disposition flagged the statistical-honesty weighting of this nuanced decision as opus-warranted at the boundary, matching S1-S3; operator started this session on opus and confirmed the final call). DECISION = DEMOTE, transition-guarded, operator-confirmed. PROCESS: routed the keep/demote/retire question through the decision-time-consensus mechanism (docs/ai-led-session-workflow.md) with the bias-cautions preamble ALWAYS ON + the devil's-advocate TWO-PASS pattern (this decision binds a long-lived workflow contract). Consulted the TWO NON-orchestrator providers (gpt-5-4 + gemini-pro; orchestrator is Anthropic/opus and per-session routed verification is what that orchestrator uses to check its OWN work, so an independent-provider consult is the correct instrument). Pass A = neutral 3-option; Pass B = steelman the contrarian hypothesis that the experiments UNDERSTATE routed's value and KEEP is correct. RESULT: ALL FOUR consults converge on DEMOTE, and BOTH Pass B passes built the strongest honest KEEP case then EXPLICITLY found it NOT decisive (raw outputs in routed-fate-consensus/, one journal record per call in routed-fate-consensus-journal.jsonl, consult spend ~$0.167). No material disagreement -> applied per the decision tree, then the OPERATOR confirmed DEMOTE via AskUserQuestion. The decision faithfully rests on: Experiment A (capability case for routed-as-default ruled out; lever is repository CONTEXT-ACCESS, which path-aware provides and snippet-fed routed structurally cannot; 2nd routed provider buys nothing) + Experiment B (pre-registered CADENCE DEFENSE DOES NOT HOLD via clause B3 -- R's edge over end-of-set is largely surface-coverage artifact + pure earliness, NOT Q-invisible cadence; explicitly NOT the B1 'no cadence value' reading, which is false -- the cadence MECHANISM is real: R catches migrating cross-file coupling defects at introduction 5/5 both providers; capability at close E 11-12/12 >> R 10/12 >> Q 4/12; path-aware E NOT a perfect ceiling, one provider's E missed Critical BD6). TWO consult-added refinements adopted: (1) TRANSITION GUARD -- do NOT hard-cut until the S5 contract-test gate is live (don't remove a working guardrail before its replacement floor exists); (2) PROGRAMMATIC gating predicate (diff-based blast-radius/coupling heuristic), NOT a subjective per-session 'is this risky?' judgment (the most dangerous sessions are the ones whose risk is under-recognized). IMPLEMENTED: routed-fate-decision.md (consensus + operator rationale, demote target-state layered-defense model, transition guard table, gating heuristic, reopen-RETIRE telemetry criteria); a 'Verification-surface policy (Set 068)' subsection + a Step 6 forward-pointer added to docs/ai-led-session-workflow.md; a comment-only anchor added to ai_router/router-config.yaml verification: block. CRITICAL: NO behavioral flag flipped and NO per-session verification skipped -- per the transition guard, per-session routed stays MANDATORY on every Full-tier session (including S4's own close-out) until the S5 gate lands; S5 builds the gate + the diff seam, S6 wires the predicate + flips the default. This honors the spec's 'routed status changes ONLY here and ONLY on the Experiment B evidence': S4 makes + records the decision and writes the policy; the operational cut-over is sequenced to S5/S6 by the operator-confirmed decision itself. VERIFICATION: gpt-5-4 (cross-provider, different from opus orchestrator) Round 1 CLEAN -> VERIFIED, zero issues (complexity 69, not escalated, ~$0.104); the up-front conventions block (suite baseline + no-behavioral-cutover-by-design + B3-not-B1 framing) per L-064-10 kept R1 focused. ai_router pytest suite 1548 passed / 1 skipped (UNCHANGED; the only ai_router/ change is a comment-only config anchor -- no production logic touched; docs/ scripts not collected). NO release (S6). Metered ~$0.274 total (consult ~$0.167 + verification $0.104 + next-orch $0.003). next-orch routed=claude-sonnet-4-6/high (S5 contract-test/CDC gate is substantial multi-file Python touching the shared close-out path).

reason.specifics: Routed analysis (route task_type=analysis) recommends claude-sonnet-4-6/high for Session 5 ('Contract-test / CDC gate'). S5 is SUBSTANTIAL multi-file Python implementation: a new deterministic contract-test/CDC gate module (runs a set's declared contract/falsifier tests as the cheap reproducible floor + reports coverage of the seeded/known defect classes, reserving the path-aware agent for the non-probeable residual), a set-level contract-declaration surface, wiring into close_session.py and/or router-config.yaml mirroring the Set 066 path-aware gate posture model (hard-block TTY / soft-warn headless), and a real test suite. sonnet-4-6 is the cheapest-CAPABLE orchestrator for multi-file Python at lower cost than opus; high effort is warranted because the gate touches the SHARED close-out path (a buggy gate could wrongly block or wrongly pass set close-outs -- real blast radius). It avoids the L-067-1 gpt-5.4-as-orchestrator budget-exhaustion risk on the agentic/implementation work while keeping gpt-5.4 + gemini available as the cross-provider verifier. CAVEAT for the S5 operator: S5 is the close-out-path wiring whose posture model the Set 066 gate established -- read close_session.py's path-aware/dedicated_verification gate wiring before mirroring it; and S5 builds the diff-inspection seam the Set 068 DEMOTE gating predicate (S6) will consume, so design that seam with the predicate in mind.
